title:
Evaluation of the Effect of Casein Phosphopeptide – Amorphous Calcium Phosphate on

Fracture Resistance of Single Rooted EDTA Treated Teeth (An in Vitro Study).
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"uhh61","text":"Aim: to evaluate the effect of CPP-ACP on fracture resistance of single rooted EDTA treated teeth when used as a final irrigant.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2s299","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"coxn6","text":"Methodology: A twenty one single rooted teeth will be randomly divided into three equal groups based upon the final irrigation protocol as follows: Group I (EDTA / CPP-ACP) – final irrigation with EDTA 17% for 1 min","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdetk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4xh1u","text":"followed by irrigation with CPP-ACP remineralizing agent for 10 min. Group II (EDTA alone) – final irrigation with EDTA 17% for 1 min. Group III (normal saline) – final irrigation with normal saline (0.9% NaCl) for 5 min. Then, Specimens from all groups will be prepared for test assembly. The apical 5 mm of roots will be embedded along the long axis in self-curing acrylic blocks, with 8 mm of each root exposed. The fracture resistance of the specimen will be tested using a Universal Testing Machine.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"unfre","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: The crown of each tooth will be sectioned perpendicular to the long axis of the root below the cementoenamel junction using a high speed diamond bur under a water coolant so that the length of the root will be adjusted to 13 mm. The working length of each root will be determined by inserting #10 K-file until it just exits the apical foramen, and then 1 mm will be subtracted from the obtained length.

The root canals will be prepared with the hyflex rotary system until file size 40 taper 4% using a gentle in and out motion with a torque-controlled endodontic motor at a constant speed of 350 rpm and torque of 1.5 N.cm. During biomechanical preparation, the canals will be irrigated with 5 ml of 5.25% sodium hypochlorite solution prior to instrumentation, 5 ml after each instrument change and 5ml as final flush after root canal instrumentation to reach a total volume of 25 ml using 30-gauge, side-vented needle placed at 1 ml from the apex
The root samples will be randomly divided into three groups based upon the final irrigation protocol as follows:

Group I (EDTA / CPP-ACP) – final irrigation with EDTA 17% for 1 min followed by irrigation with CPP-ACP remineralizing agent for 10 min. -Remineralizing solution will be prepared by diluting the GC Tooth Mousse to 10 times with distilled water17. Fracture resistance will be measured after the final irrigation protocol.
Group II (EDTA alone) – final irrigation with EDTA 17% for 1 min.
Group III (normal saline) – final irrigation with normal saline (0.9% NaCl) for 5 min.

After chemo-mechanical preparation, each sample will be irrigated with 5 ml EDTA 17% for 1 minute, then washed thoroughly with normal saline, followed by 10 ml of the previously prepared remineralizing solution for 10 minutes, then washed adequately with normal saline.
After chemo-mechanical preparation, each sample will be irrigated with 5 ml EDTA 17% for 1 minute, then washed thoroughly with normal saline.
After chemo-mechanical preparation, final irrigation with 10 ml normal saline for 5 minutes.
Specimens from all groups will be prepared for test assembly. The apical 5 mm of roots will be embedded along the long axis in self-curing acrylic blocks, with 8 mm of each root exposed

The fracture resistance of the specimen will be tested using a Universal Testing Machine (Lloyd, LR50K, UK). A custommade stainless steel loading indenter with a round tip (r = 2 mm) will be used to deliver force directed along the long axis of the tooth. The load will be increased at a rate of 1 mm/min until fracture occurs. The fracture will be evidenced by an audible crack and/or a sudden drop in load. The force necessary to fracture the root will be recorded in Newtons (N).
